nodes.label: P15_T001
nodes.identity_summary: A practical, value-conscious student from Ahmedabad who prioritizes price and proven efficacy over marketing hype. They rely heavily on online research, checking multiple review platforms and ingredient lists before buying. While open to trying new products with validation, they're unwilling to pay premiums for unproven claims—only clinical evidence or expert dermatologist recommendations can justify higher costs.

nodes.label: Relatable-Content Seeker
nodes.dimension: Preference for aspirational versus relatable advertising content

nodes.label: Expert-Trusting Skeptic
nodes.dimension: Credibility attributed to celebrity versus expert endorsements

nodes.label: price-first-decision-maker
nodes.description: Consistently prioritizes price as the #1 factor when selecting personal care products, often choosing the cheaper option unless there's clear evidence of superior benefits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: ["price","recommendation","ingredients","brand","expert","fragrance","reviews","packaging"]
[REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? 100_200
[SYNTHETIC] Pick Brand B at ₹199 for better ingredients and lower price.
[SYNTHETIC] Switch to the ₹249 alternative without much thought.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium-priced body wash without clinical proof, this person would likely reject it in favor of cheaper alternatives.

nodes.label: expert-recommendation-trusts-over-social-influence
nodes.description: Values recommendations from dermatologists and skincare experts far more than celebrity endorsements, social media influencers, or even friend recommendations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. Splainer, sjbreddits
[REAL] What is it about their content or approach that makes you trust their recommendations over others? Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity
[BRANCH] I'd be more influenced by Influencer A. As someone who trusts skincare expertise, I value recommendations from professionals for clinical benefits.
[SYNTHETIC] Trust points allocation: Dermatologist's Instagram post 4, YouTube review by creator 3, Reddit thread 1, Mother's recommendation 0.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a dermatologist explaining pH balance, this person would be far more likely to engage than with a celebrity-endorsed ad.

nodes.label: multi-platform-review-checker
nodes.description: Doesn't trust reviews from a single source; cross-checks products across Amazon, Nykaa, Reddit, and other platforms before purchasing.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] If there are conflicting claims, search for more reviews on other platforms.
[SYNTHETIC] When on Blinkit with limited details, pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first.
[SYNTHETIC] Check another platform for more authentic reviews if reviews seem incentivized.
[REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["amazon","nykaa","kirana"]
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product with high ratings on one platform, this person would still verify on other sites before buying.

nodes.label: clinical-evidence-justifies-premium
nodes.description: Willing to pay significantly more (up to 2x) only for products with clinically proven, dermatologist-validated benefits that improve skin health.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? Clinically proven positive effects on skin health
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ["clinical_studies","derm_recommended","intense_moisture"]
[REAL] Maximum premium for clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation: 10_20%
[SYNTHETIC] I'd need to see the clinical study results first before paying more for a derm-recommended version.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash with published clinical trial results, this person would consider paying a premium price.

nodes.label: natural-claims-skeptical-unless-harm-proven
nodes.description: Views 'natural' and 'chemical-free' as largely irrelevant marketing terms unless there's a known harmful effect from specific ingredients.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do these qualities influence your decision when choosing beauty products if at all? Irrelevant unless a known harmful effect exists
[REAL] When it comes to beauty products, do you see a difference between these qualities and being 'chemical-free'? No
[REAL] How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? {"rating":3,"explanation":"Locally sourced, fresh and homemade"}
[SYNTHETIC] Continue as before since the distinction doesn't matter to me when told most 'natural' claims are marketing.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash marketed as '100% natural' without evidence of superior efficacy, this person would dismiss it.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-verifier
nodes.description: Actively reads and verifies ingredient lists, recognizing key components like SLS and checking for claimed-free certifications.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list when seeing 'Paraben-Free' claim.
[SYNTHETIC] Recognize a few key ones like SLS when reading ingredient lists.
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free
[SYNTHETIC] Research the ingredient to understand the distinction when there's a discrepancy between claim and ingredient list.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product claiming 'sulfate-free,' this person would flip the bottle to verify before believing the claim.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-for-immediacy-not-discovery
nodes.description: Uses Blinkit/Zepto for urgent needs when almost out of product, but prefers traditional online platforms for research and better selection.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you use quick commerce for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? Yes, yes
[REAL] Can you share a recent example where the limited selection on these platforms influenced your choice? Bought a smaller skincare unit as a temporary fix; desired product unavailable
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Blinkit at ₹299 for immediate delivery since I'm almost out.
[SYNTHETIC] Pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery when needed today.
nodes.predictive_value: When discovering a new body wash, this person would research on Nykaa/Amazon first, not buy impulsively from quick commerce.

nodes.label: relatable-context-responder
nodes.description: Engages more with advertising showing realistic, everyday scenarios (student in hostel) versus aspirational luxury imagery.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd engage more with Ad B showing the student in the hostel bathroom. It feels genuine and relevant to my daily life as a student in Ahmedabad.
[REAL] How would you describe your personality in 3-5 words? Friendly and easygoing
[SYNTHETIC] Prefer dermatologist campaign over Bollywood celebrity campaign—trust expert credibility more.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a relatable student routine, this person would be more likely to watch and consider the product.

nodes.label: brand-origin-agnostic
nodes.description: Believes brand origin (Indian vs. international) is a marketing gimmick and focuses instead on formulation and effectiveness.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? No preference
[REAL] Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice? Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the 'Made in India' one at ₹249 over imported at ₹449 when ingredients are similar.
[SYNTHETIC] No change in perception if brand gets acquired by conglomerate—as long as product is same.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product emphasizing 'Imported from Korea,' this person would not pay a premium unless formulation is demonstrably better.

nodes.label: discount-triggered-trial
nodes.description: Highly responsive to discounts and flash sales, ranking them as top purchase triggers for trying new products.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Rank these purchase triggers: 1) 50% off flash sale on Nykaa, 2) Dermatologist's recommendation, 3) Free sample sachets
[REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: Discount,expert_review
[SYNTHETIC] The discount is tempting enough to try something new over an ad reinforcing current choice.
[SYNTHETIC] Rank discount presentations: '₹100 off — now ₹199' as most appealing.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a 50% off flash sale for a new body wash, this person would likely try it even if unfamiliar with the brand.

nodes.label: sample-before-commitment
nodes.description: Prefers trying samples or travel sizes before committing to full-size purchases, especially for higher-priced items.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Free sample sachets are a practical trigger for trying without full commitment.
[SYNTHETIC] Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size after a bad experience with another new brand.
[SYNTHETIC] Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size to test before committing.
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the sachet at local kirana to try—perfect low-risk trial.
nodes.predictive_value: When offered a free sample sachet with purchase, this person would be more likely to add the product to cart.

nodes.label: practical-format-preference
nodes.description: Prefers body wash over bar soap for ease of use, values essential lather, lightweight texture, and minimalist packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you currently use body wash, bar soap, or both? What drives that choice? Body wash due to ease of use
[REAL] How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? Essential
[REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? Lightweight
[REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: Minimalist
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash with a unique dispensing mechanism but impractical format, this person would likely stick with traditional pump bottles.

nodes.label: fragrance-shower-only-expectation
nodes.description: Expects body wash fragrance to last only during the shower, not on skin throughout the day, and prefers oceanic/aquatic or sweet gourmand scents.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? Shower_only
[REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: Ocean_aquatic,sweet_gourmand
[REAL] Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved. Titan Skinn Verge. Simple, safe scent; long-lasting
[SYNTHETIC] Use regular body wash and layer with perfume/deo for wedding instead of buying new fragrant body wash.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash claiming '24-hour fragrance,' this person would be skeptical and not value that claim.

nodes.label: value-over-packaging
nodes.description: Chooses basic packaging at lower price over sleek, premium packaging when product performance is similar.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249 over sleek minimalist bottle at ₹399 when identical.
[SYNTHETIC] Skip limited edition designer packaging—paying for formula, not bottle.
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? Easy to use, cheap, smells okay
[SYNTHETIC] Not bother me — I'm paying for the formulation, not the factory when same factory makes budget and premium.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown two identical products where one has premium packaging at higher cost, this person would choose the basic version.

nodes.label: sustainability-premium-limited
nodes.description: Willing to pay a moderate premium (20-40%) for sustainable packaging or cruelty-free certification, but not for Ayurvedic or 'Made in India' claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values: {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"20_40","Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"0","Cruelty-free, not tested on animals":"20_40"}
[SYNTHETIC] About ₹30-40 justified for sustainability claims out of ₹80 difference.
[REAL] Which certifications influence purchase? Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash with eco-friendly packaging at 30% premium, this person would consider it; at 50% premium, they'd likely reject it.

nodes.label: online-browsing-default
nodes.description: Default behavior when product runs out is to browse online across platforms rather than immediately repurchasing the same item.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? Browse_online
[REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["amazon","nykaa","kirana"]
[SYNTHETIC] Allocate shopping time: Compare prices across platforms 12 min, read text reviews 8 min.
[SYNTHETIC] Would drive to DMart to save ₹50 over buying on Nykaa right now.
nodes.predictive_value: When reminded to repurchase a running-out product, this person would still browse alternatives online before deciding.

nodes.label: switch-for-practical-improvements
nodes.description: Switches brands primarily for practical reasons like better packaging, ease of use, or doctor recommendations—not for fragrance or trendiness.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. Switched from clumsy option for better packaging and ease of use. Satisfied
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ["doctor_rec","friend_rec","cheaper"]
[SYNTHETIC] Try D2C brand friend recommended if current brand increases price.
[REAL] What frustrates you about current body wash? Refill packs are not economical.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash with innovative, leakproof travel packaging, this person would be more likely to switch than for a new fragrance variant.

nodes.label: reddit-community-trust
nodes.description: Trusts Reddit communities and independent reviewers (like Splainer) for unbiased, detailed reviews more than mainstream influencers.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space? Splainer, sjbreddits
[REAL] Which social media platform did you see the product on? Reddit. Multiple positive comments
[SYNTHETIC] The Reddit India thread with real user before/after photos influences more than brand's clinical trial claim.
[SYNTHETIC] More likely to try Reddit-recommended body wash over influencer-promoted one at same price.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash with strong Reddit community endorsement, this person would trust it more than an Instagram influencer review.

nodes.label: skin-dryness-conscious
nodes.description: Has skin dryness concerns and seeks products that foam well without drying skin, especially for Indian skin types and hard water conditions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. Dryness
[REAL] What specific benefits would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing? Foams well, does not dry skin
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? Neutral
[REAL] Interested in body wash for Indian skin/hard water: rating 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash specifically formulated for hard water that prevents dryness, this person would be highly interested.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-routine
nodes.description: Bathing and grooming routine varies with mood rather than being fixed, suggesting flexibility in product usage patterns.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? Mood_dependent
[SYNTHETIC] Use premium body wash for special occasions, regular for daily use after receiving gift.
[REAL] How many times a day do you typically shower/bathe? Once
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash marketed for 'energizing morning showers' or 'calming evening baths,' this person might use it situationally based on mood.

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-weighed
nodes.description: Values friend recommendations but verifies them with independent research rather than blindly following.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Ask friend what specifically they like about product before switching on their recommendation.
[REAL] What would make you switch? friend_rec is in top 3
[SYNTHETIC] Trust friend's thorough research-oriented recommendation over style-admired friend's suggestion.
[SYNTHETIC] Look up brand on Amazon/Nykaa and read strangers' reviews too when friends buzz about it.
nodes.predictive_value: When a close friend recommends a body wash, this person would research it online before purchasing rather than buying immediately.

nodes.label: subscription-averse-preferences-freedom
nodes.description: Prefers flexibility to switch brands over subscription discounts, avoiding locked-in commitments.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Don't subscribe to Subscribe & Save—like freedom to switch brands anytime.
[SYNTHETIC] Choose Amazon subscription with 5% off and flexible cancellation over brand's 15% off with lock-in.
[SYNTHETIC] Skip mystery body wash subscription box—don't want random products.
[SYNTHETIC] Choose Plan A (same variant auto-delivery) over Plan B (surprise variant) for subscription.
nodes.predictive_value: When offered a subscription with significant discount but commitment, this person would likely decline in favor of one-time purchase.

nodes.label: social-proof-volume-over-rating
nodes.description: Trusts products with high review counts (e.g., 15,000 reviews) more than those with few reviews even if rating is higher.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews trusted more than Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews.
[REAL] When shopping online, what do you look at first? ["price","review_count","brand_name"]
[SYNTHETIC] Click on product with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average over one with 85 ratings and 4.8 average.
[SYNTHETIC] Buy from Nykaa with 2,000+ reviews at 4.5 stars over Amazon with 50 reviews at 4.2 even if ₹40 cheaper.
nodes.predictive_value: When comparing two products, this person would choose the one with substantially more reviews even if average rating is slightly lower.

nodes.label: travel-size-convenience-over-value
nodes.description: Chooses travel sizes for convenience during trips despite higher per-ml cost, but not for regular use.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Buy sachet when traveling and need something small.
[SYNTHETIC] Pick 100ml travel size at ₹129 for 2-week need over 250ml at ₹249 on quick commerce.
[REAL] What frustrates you about current body wash? Refill packs are not economical
[SYNTHETIC] Buy local store's unfamiliar brand for ₹99 as temporary fix when traveling.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a travel-sized body wash at airport prices, this person would buy a cheaper local option instead.

nodes.label: innovation-skeptical-until-proven
nodes.description: Interested in innovative formats (solid bars, waterless concentrates) but waits for them to appear on mainstream platforms with reviews before trying.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Wait for solid body wash bar to appear on Amazon/Nykaa with reviews before trying.
[SYNTHETIC] Try waterless body wash if sample available but wouldn't commit upfront.
[SYNTHETIC] Primary hesitation about bakuchiol body wash: 'Body wash washes off—would the ingredient even work?'
[SYNTHETIC] Intrigued by probiotic body wash but would Google and probably need dermatologist recommendation.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a novel body wash format, this person would wait for it to be available on Amazon with substantial reviews before purchasing.

nodes.label: P15_T002
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a value-driven, research-oriented student who prioritizes functional benefits (lather, skin feel, ease of use) and clinical validation over brand prestige or aesthetic packaging. They are socially conscious about scent in close-contact gatherings and rely on expert recommendations (dermatologists, independent journalists) and peer reviews when trying new products. While open to novelty, they proceed cautiously, comparing prices and ingredients online, and will pay a premium only for proven skin health benefits or verified sustainability claims.

nodes.label: Aspirational Dreamer
nodes.dimension: Preference for aspirational versus relatable advertising content

nodes.label: Balanced Practical Buyer
nodes.dimension: Credibility attributed to celebrity versus expert endorsements

nodes.label: compares-prices-and-reviews-before-purchase
nodes.description: Actively compares prices and reads multiple reviews across platforms before making a purchase, especially for new products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? Browse_online
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: ["price","review_count","brand_name"]
[SYNTHETIC] I'd check the Shark Tank brand's reviews on my phone before deciding.
[SYNTHETIC] Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash ad with a discount, this person would likely not buy immediately but instead check multiple platforms for reviews and price comparisons.

nodes.label: trusts-clinical-expertise-over-celebrity-endorsement
nodes.description: Places higher trust in recommendations from dermatologists, clinical studies, and independent journalistic sources than in celebrity endorsements or influencer marketing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ["clinical_studies","derm_recommended","intense_moisture"]
[REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Splainer, sjbreddits - Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity
[BRANCH] Rank these five purchase triggers from most to least likely... 1) Dermatologist's recommendation – expert validation matters for skin health.
[SYNTHETIC] The dermatologist campaign — I trust expert credibility more.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a dermatologist explaining pH balance or clinical results, this person would be significantly more likely to engage and consider purchase than an ad with a celebrity.

nodes.label: function-over-form-seeker
nodes.description: Prioritizes functional benefits like lather, texture, skin feel, and ease of use over aesthetic aspects like packaging or long-lasting fragrance.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? Essential
[REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? Lightweight
[REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? Shower_only
[REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... Watery texture, foams directly via dispenser, non-drying but effective clean, leakproof for travel, cheap
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept emphasizing luxurious packaging or 24-hour fragrance, this person would be less impressed unless it also clearly communicates superior lather, lightweight feel, and skin compatibility.

nodes.label: social-gathering-scent-conscious
nodes.description: Particularly conscious of body odor and fragrance in social gatherings with close contact, wanting to avoid bothering others and make a positive impression.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When you think about how your scent affects others, is there a particular situation where you're more conscious of it? Social gatherings with close contact where body odor may bother others.

[BRANCH] I'd be drawn to Ad A with the celebrity at the luxury party. It makes the product feel special and desirable, which appeals to me for social gatherings where I want to smell good and make an impression.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd use a special/premium body wash I reserve for important occasions.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad that visualizes a close-contact social scenario (party, wedding, group selfie) and highlights freshness and confidence, this person would be more likely to pay attention and recall the product.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-for-immediate-needs
nodes.description: Turns to quick commerce platforms (Blinkit/Zepto) for immediate needs, accepting limited selection and sometimes higher prices to solve an urgent problem.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? Yes, yes
[REAL] Can you share a recent example where the limited selection on these platforms influenced your choice? Bought a smaller skincare unit as a temporary fix; desired product unavailable
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Blinkit for immediate need despite higher price.
[SYNTHETIC] Pick from Zepto’s limited range for instant delivery when I need it today.
nodes.predictive_value: When running a promotion on quick commerce apps with messaging like 'Running out? Get it in 10 mins', this person would be a likely converter during stock-out moments.

nodes.label: ingredient-checker-for-harmful-substances
nodes.description: Checks ingredient lists for specific substances like sulfates and parabens, and values corresponding certifications, but is not dogmatically 'natural'—only avoids known harmful effects.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do these qualities [natural/chemical-free] influence your decision when choosing beauty products if at all? Irrelevant unless a known harmful effect exists
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free
[SYNTHETIC] Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list.
[SYNTHETIC] Look for sulfate-free options and compare multiple brands.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product page or label with 'Sulfate-Free' or 'Paraben-Free' badges prominently displayed, this person would be more likely to pause and consider the product as a safer choice.

nodes.label: brand-origin-agnostic
nodes.description: Believes brand origin (Indian vs. international) is a marketing gimmick and does not influence purchase decisions, focusing instead on formulation and effectiveness.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? No preference
[REAL] Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice? Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same
[SYNTHETIC] Not bother me — I'm paying for the formulation, not the factory.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad that leans heavily on 'Made in India' or 'International Luxury' as a primary appeal, this person would be skeptical and look for more substantive claims about performance or ingredients.

nodes.label: value-maximizer-for-sustainability
nodes.description: Willing to pay a moderate premium (20-40%) for sustainability (eco-friendly packaging, cruelty-free) but assigns no extra value to 'Ayurvedic', 'traditional', or 'Made in India' claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"20_40","Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"0","Cruelty-free, not tested on animals":"20_40","Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation":"10_20","Made in India":"0"}
[SYNTHETIC] About ₹30-40 — I value it but not that much [for sustainability donation].
[SYNTHETIC] Invest in reusable glass bottle and refills to save money and reduce plastic waste.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash with credible eco-certifications (e.g., recyclable packaging, Leaping Bunny) at a 25% premium, this person would consider it a justifiable upgrade, but an 'Ayurvedic' claim at a premium would be dismissed.

nodes.label: dry-skin-focused
nodes.description: Has dryness as a primary skin concern and seeks products that provide intense moisture, do not exacerbate dryness, and are clinically proven to improve skin health.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. Dryness
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? intense_moisture
[REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? Clinically proven positive effects on skin health
[REAL] What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? Foams well, does not dry skin
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a body wash concept that features 'intense moisture', '72-hour hydration', or 'clinically proven to reduce dryness', this person would be highly motivated to read details and consider a trial.

nodes.label: discount-and-deal-responsive
nodes.description: Actively looks for and is influenced by discounts, cashback, and bundle deals, often using them to justify trying new products or stocking up.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: Discount,expert_review
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the combo to save money even if I don't need the second now.
[SYNTHETIC] Switch to cheaper body wash ₹149 and keep serum [to cut cart cost].
[SYNTHETIC] Buy from cashback store if I plan to shop there again.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a limited-time flash sale (e.g., '50% off for 6 hours') or a bundle offer ('Buy 2, Get 1 free'), this person would be more likely to make an impulsive purchase or stock up.

nodes.label: prefers-online-channels-for-research-and-purchase
nodes.description: Prefers to research, compare, and purchase body wash through online platforms like Amazon and Nykaa, valuing selection, reviews, and convenience over physical stores.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["amazon","nykaa","kirana"]
[REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? Browse_online
[SYNTHETIC] Buy on Nykaa for more reviews and higher rating despite ₹40 extra.
[SYNTHETIC] Click the Amazon link for easier checkout and trusted platform.
nodes.predictive_value: When launching a new body wash, ensuring strong presence with detailed images, ingredient lists, and reviews on Amazon and Nykaa is critical to capture this person's consideration.

nodes.label: open-to-trying-new-brands-with-caution
nodes.description: Comfortable trying new brands but does so cautiously, often relying on trusted recommendations, positive reviews, or low-risk trial sizes before committing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? 4
[SYNTHETIC] Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size.
[SYNTHETIC] After it gets listed on Amazon/Nykaa with 100+ reviews.
[SYNTHETIC] Browse their site but probably won't buy unless something really stands out.
nodes.predictive_value: When introducing a new brand, offering a low-cost trial sachet or a 'first-order discount' coupled with strong review numbers would significantly increase this person's likelihood to try.

nodes.label: values-friend-and-family-recommendations
nodes.description: Values personal recommendations from friends and family, considering them a credible source that can trigger a switch from current products.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ["doctor_rec","friend_rec","cheaper"]
[REAL] Where do you typically discover new personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["magazine_blog","friends_family","youtube"]
[SYNTHETIC] Choose the one recommended by my close friend.
[SYNTHETIC] Ask them what they've seen on social media and help evaluate [for sibling].
nodes.predictive_value: A referral program or a campaign encouraging users to 'Recommend to a Friend' with a shared discount would be effective in driving purchases from this person and their network.

nodes.label: minimalist-packaging-preference
nodes.description: Prefers minimalist packaging design and does not value ornate or designer packaging enough to pay a significant premium for it.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: Minimalist
[SYNTHETIC] Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249, packaging not worth premium.
[SYNTHETIC] Skip it — I'm paying for the product, not the bottle.
[SYNTHETIC] Brand A — same product, why pay more? [re: premium packaging]
nodes.predictive_value: When designing packaging, a clean, simple, and functional design will be more appealing than an overly decorative or luxury-themed one, especially if it keeps costs down.

nodes.label: P15_T003
nodes.identity_summary: P15_T003 is a pragmatic, price-conscious student shopper who balances a strong value orientation with a need for proven efficacy. Their purchase decisions are fundamentally driven by price comparisons and tangible benefits (especially dermatologist validation), while being highly skeptical of marketing gimmicks and indifferent to brand origin or natural claims. They are a selective researcher who trusts community-driven reviews (like Reddit) over traditional influencers, and their mood-dependent grooming routine means contextual triggers (like stress or social events) significantly influence their engagement with products.

nodes.label: Context-Sensitive Engager
nodes.dimension: Preference for aspirational versus relatable advertising content

nodes.label: Social-Proof Oriented
nodes.dimension: Credibility attributed to celebrity versus expert endorsements

nodes.label: price-value-calculator-foremost
nodes.description: Price is the single most important factor in any purchase decision, but they perform a quick mental calculation of value-for-money, often comparing per-ml costs and immediate discounts.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q11: When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: ["price","recommendation","ingredients","brand","expert","fragrance","reviews","packaging"]
[REAL] Q21: How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 100_200
[SYNTHETIC] Q79: Allocate 100 points across these five factors... Price/deal available right now: 40
[SYNTHETIC] Q90: If you had to choose between these two deals... Pick Deal B: ₹259 body wash without freebies for better price.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a premium-priced concept, this person would immediately compare it to their ₹100-200 benchmark and require clear, tangible justification for the price difference.

nodes.label: dermatologist-validation-seeker
nodes.description: Actively seeks and highly values recommendations from dermatologists or clinical claims, viewing them as the gold standard for product efficacy and safety.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q50: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ["clinical_studies","derm_recommended","intense_moisture"]
[REAL] Q54: What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health
[BRANCH] Q61: Rank these five purchase triggers... 1) Dermatologist's recommendation – expert validation matters for skin health.
[SYNTHETIC] Q96: ...I'd need to see the clinical study results first.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a dermatologist or clinical study data, this person would be significantly more likely to stop, read, and consider the product.

nodes.label: brand-origin-skeptic
nodes.description: Firmly believes that brand origin (Indian vs. International) is a marketing gimmick and that production processes are usually the same, making it irrelevant to their choice.

[REAL] Q17: Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice...? A: Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same
[REAL] Q18: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... "Made in India":"0"
[SYNTHETIC] Q84: Buy the ₹249 'Made in India' product, ignoring the imported label.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product concept heavily leaning on 'International' or 'Imported' as a key benefit, this person would dismiss it as irrelevant and focus on price and functional benefits instead.

nodes.label: community-review-trust-over-influencer
nodes.description: Trusts aggregated, community-driven reviews (Reddit, Amazon with high review counts) more than individual influencer endorsements, valuing the lack of perceived incentive to lie.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q56: Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones... A: Splainer, sjbreddits
[REAL] Q57: What is it about their content or approach that makes you trust their recommendations over others? A: Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity
[REAL] Q60: Where do you typically discover new personal care products? Pick your top 3: ["magazine_blog","friends_family","youtube"]
[SYNTHETIC] Q93: Brand A with 4.2 stars and 15,000 reviews at ₹279.
[SYNTHETIC] Q284: The Reddit-recommended one [over the influencer-promoted one].
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product launch driven by influencer marketing, this person would seek out community reviews on Reddit or Amazon before considering a purchase, distrusting the paid promotion.

nodes.label: mood-context-sensitive-engager
nodes.description: Their receptiveness to advertising and product exploration is heavily dependent on their immediate context and mood (e.g., studying vs. relaxing), not just the ad content itself.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q32: How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent

[BRANCH] Q63: It depends on my mood and what I need at the time. Sometimes Ad A is inspiring and fun, especially if I'm planning for a social event. Other times, Ad B is more useful for my daily routine.
[BRANCH] Q65: It's completely different! When I'm studying, ads are just distractions—I'd scroll past without a second thought. At home, I'm in a better mood to explore...
nodes.predictive_value: When targeting this person with digital ads, timing (e.g., evening leisure hours) will be as crucial as messaging, as stress or focus blocks all marketing influence.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-substitution-accepter
nodes.description: Willing to accept a temporary substitute or a more expensive option when purchasing from quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto) due to urgency and limited selection, viewing it as a separate, convenience-driven channel.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q25: Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, yes

[REAL] Q26: Can you share a recent example where the limited selection on these platforms influenced your choice of personal care product? A: Bought a smaller skincare unit as a temporary fix; desired product unavailable
[SYNTHETIC] Q69: Buy from Blinkit at ₹299 for quick delivery since I'm almost out.
[SYNTHETIC] Q188: Pick the cheapest option — it's a temporary substitute anyway.
nodes.predictive_value: When out of product, they will prioritize immediate convenience over brand loyalty or optimal price, making them a prime target for top-listed brands on quick-commerce apps.

nodes.label: natural-claims-ambivalent
nodes.description: Has a moderate interest in 'natural' (defined as locally sourced, fresh) but is unconcerned with the chemical-free vs. natural distinction and will not pay a premium for Ayurvedic or natural claims alone.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q12: How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? ... A: {"rating":3,"explanation":"Locally sourced, fresh and homemade"}
[REAL] Q13: When it comes to beauty products, do you see a difference between these qualities and being 'chemical-free'? A: No
[REAL] Q18: ... "Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"0"
[SYNTHETIC] Q150: ...continue as before since the distinction doesn't matter to you.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product leading with '100% Natural' or 'Ayurvedic' as its primary claim, this person would not be swayed unless paired with a strong price advantage or proven efficacy.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-scanner-not-deep-researcher
nodes.description: Scans ingredient lists to recognize key flagged ingredients (like SLS, parabens) but does not conduct deep research into every component; trusts certifications and claims as a shortcut.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q15: Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free
[SYNTHETIC] Q109: Accept the claim and feel good about it.
[SYNTHETIC] Q114: Recognize a few key ones like SLS.
[SYNTHETIC] Q121: Notice only if the product felt different on my skin.
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a product page,他们会先看认证标签（如无硫酸盐），然后快速浏览成分表找已知关键词，但不会花时间研究陌生成分，除非产品引起皮肤问题。

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-weighted
nodes.description: Values recommendations from close friends and family, seeing them as credible due to personal trust, but will still cross-check with their own research or reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q22: What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ["doctor_rec","friend_rec","cheaper"]
[SYNTHETIC] Q106: Choose the body wash my close friend loves.
[SYNTHETIC] Q155: Try the D2C brand your friend recommended.
[SYNTHETIC] Q265: Look it up on Amazon/Nykaa and read reviews from strangers too.
nodes.predictive_value: A strong personal recommendation from a close friend would prompt them to research the product, acting as a powerful initial trigger they are likely to act upon.

nodes.label: functional-fragrance-preference
nodes.description: Prefers simple, safe, long-lasting scents (ocean/aquatic, sweet gourmand) and does not expect or want body wash fragrance to last on skin beyond the shower; fragrance is a nice-to-have, not a core benefit.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q35: What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Ocean_aquatic,sweet_gourmand
[REAL] Q36: Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved... A: Titan Skinn Verge. Simple, safe scent; long-lasting
[REAL] Q37: How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Shower_only
[REAL] Q44: What do you like MOST about your current body wash? ...smells okay

nodes.label: lather-essentialist
nodes.description: Considers rich lather/foam an essential, non-negotiable part of the body wash experience, associating it with effective cleansing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q38: How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Essential
[REAL] Q49: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... foams directly via dispenser
[REAL] Q53: What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? A: Foams well
nodes.predictive_value: Any product concept or ad that visually demonstrates or claims abundant, rich lather would directly appeal to this person's core expectation of what makes a body wash effective.

nodes.label: pragmatic-packaging-preference
nodes.description: Prefers minimalist, functional packaging (like a dispenser for foam) that is leak-proof for travel; sees decorative or premium packaging as an unjustified cost.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q41: Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist
[REAL] Q49: ...leakproof for travel, cheap

[SYNTHETIC] Q161: Skip it — I'm paying for the product, not the bottle.

nodes.label: sustainability-premium-calculator
nodes.description: Willing to pay a moderate premium (20-40%) for sustainable/eco-friendly packaging or cruelty-free certification, but the value must be clear and not extreme.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q18: ... {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"20_40","Cruelty-free, not tested on animals":"20_40"}
[SYNTHETIC] Q101: About ₹30-40 — I value it but not that much.

nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a sustainable product,他们会进行快速的心算：如果价格比普通产品高出的部分在他们认可的溢价范围内（20-40%），他们会考虑；如果超出，他们会认为不值。

nodes.label: post-wash-neutral-skin-feel-seeker
nodes.description: Desires a 'neutral' post-wash skin feeling—not too dry, not too slick—indicating a preference for effective cleansing without heavy residue or stripping moisture.

[REAL] Q49: ...non-drying but effective clean
[REAL] Q53: ...does not dry skin
nodes.predictive_value: Product claims like 'non-drying', 'balanced clean', or 'skin-neutral pH' would resonate strongly, while claims of intense moisturization or oiliness might be perceived as undesirable.

nodes.label: platform-specific-behavior-segmenter
nodes.description: Exhibits distinctly different shopping behaviors depending on the platform: deep research on Amazon/Nykaa, but impulse/urgency-driven purchases on quick-commerce apps.

nodes.predictive_value: 他们会在亚马逊上研究一款产品，但最终可能在 Blinkit 上购买一个更熟悉的品牌以备急用。针对他们的营销策略需要区分平台：亚马逊上提供详细信息和评论，Blinkit 上突出便捷性和熟悉度。

nodes.label: risk-mitigator-via-small-trials
nodes.description: Mitigates the risk of trying new products by preferring small, low-cost trial sizes, sachets, or samples before committing to a full-size purchase.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q74: Buy sachet when traveling and need something small.

[SYNTHETIC] Q248: Buy the sachet to try — perfect low-risk trial.
nodes.predictive_value: A new product launch offering a ₹99 trial size or a free sample with purchase would dramatically lower their barrier to trial compared to requiring a full-price commitment.

nodes.label: deal-type-discriminator
nodes.description: Prefers simple, immediate discounts (e.g., '₹100 off') over complex or delayed-value deals (e.g., cashback, bundling), as they are easier to evaluate and provide instant gratification.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q94: Rank these discount presentations from most to least appealing: A) '₹100 off — now ₹199' ... E) 'Free shipping (worth ₹50) + ₹50 off'
[SYNTHETIC] Q162: Brand B's ₹100 cashback since I only need one bottle now.
[SYNTHETIC] Q90: Pick Deal B: ₹259 body wash without freebies for better price.
nodes.predictive_value: A straightforward discount displayed prominently on a product listing or ad will capture their attention and drive purchase more effectively than a 'buy one get one' or cashback offer.

nodes.label: P15_T004
nodes.identity_summary: P15_T004 is a value-conscious student who prioritizes functional efficacy and expert validation over branding or fragrance. They are research-driven, using multiple sources (especially dermatologist recommendations and high-volume reviews) to de-risk purchases, and are open to new products if backed by evidence. Their purchase behavior is pragmatic—favoring convenience and price in urgent situations, but planning ahead for value—and they separate product performance from marketing claims like 'natural' or brand origin.

nodes.label: expert-validation-seeker-over-influencer-hype
nodes.description: Prioritizes recommendations from dermatologists and credible experts over influencers or celebrities, valuing clinical evidence and lack of financial bias.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Trusts: Splainer, sjbreddits for lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity."
[REAL] "Claims that attract: clinical_studies, derm_recommended, intense_moisture."
[BRANCH] "I'd be more influenced by Influencer A (skincare expert)... I value recommendations from professionals for clinical benefits."
[SYNTHETIC] "Choose the dermatologist-recommended one."
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad featuring a dermatologist explaining pH balance or clinical results, this person would likely pause and engage, whereas a celebrity-endorsed ad would be ignored.

nodes.label: functional-benefit-over-fragrance-focus
nodes.description: Values practical benefits like effective cleansing, lather, and skin compatibility more than fragrance longevity or scent complexity.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? Essential."
[REAL] "What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? Clinically proven positive effects on skin health."
[REAL] "What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? Foams well, does not dry skin."

nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a new body wash concept, they would first assess claims about lather, skin feel, and clinical benefits before considering the fragrance profile.

nodes.label: high-volume-review-truster-over-niche-hype
nodes.description: Trusts products with thousands of reviews and established ratings more than those with few but stellar reviews, seeing volume as a reliability signal.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? price, review_count, brand_name."
[SYNTHETIC] "Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279 — more trustworthy due to volume and price."
[SYNTHETIC] "Click on the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first."
[SYNTHETIC] "Order the regular body wash with 15,000+ reviews. More reviews give me confidence."
nodes.predictive_value: A new product launch with only 50 glowing reviews would be approached with skepticism; they'd wait until it accumulates hundreds of reviews before seriously considering.

nodes.label: pragmatic-convenience-spender-in-urgency
nodes.description: When nearly out of a product, prioritizes immediate availability and fast delivery (quick commerce) over price savings, treating convenience as a premium worth paying for in the moment.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Uses quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) and the limited selection changes what they buy."
[SYNTHETIC] "Order from Blinkit at ₹299 for 10-minute delivery since I'm almost out."

[SYNTHETIC] "Blinkit for quick delivery despite limited selection."
nodes.predictive_value: If shown a 'low stock' alert or a quick-commerce exclusive variant, they would likely opt for the fast delivery option even at a slight price premium when replenishment is urgent.

nodes.label: planned-value-maximizer-in-non-urgency
nodes.description: When not in immediate need, actively compares prices across platforms, waits for sales, and buys larger sizes or multi-packs for better per-unit value.

[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the larger 500ml size for better value per ml."
[SYNTHETIC] "Drive to DMart to save ₹50."
[SYNTHETIC] "Annoyed; wait longer before buying next time to avoid price drops."
nodes.predictive_value: A 'subscribe & save' offer or a bulk discount would appeal to them during planned replenishment cycles, but they'd compare it to other channel prices first.

nodes.label: ingredient-label-scanner-not-dogmatist
nodes.description: Reads ingredient lists to verify claims (like 'sulfate-free') and understand formulation, but isn't driven by 'natural' dogma; focuses on known harmful effects and functional ingredients.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Certifications that influence: Cruelty_free, dermatologist_tested, sulfate_free, paraben_free."
[REAL] "Irrelevant unless a known harmful effect exists."
[SYNTHETIC] "Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list."
[SYNTHETIC] "Research the specific flagged ingredients."
nodes.predictive_value: A product page with a detailed, transparent ingredient breakdown and explanations would earn more trust than one with vague 'natural' claims.

nodes.label: brand-origin-skeptic
nodes.description: Believes brand origin (Indian vs. international) is a marketing gimmick, focusing instead on production processes and formulation efficacy.

[REAL] "No preference for Indian vs. international brands."
[REAL] "Maximum premium for 'Made in India': 0%."
[SYNTHETIC] "No change if formula stays same."
nodes.predictive_value: Ads emphasizing 'Swiss formulation' or 'Indian Ayurvedic heritage' would not sway them unless paired with concrete evidence of superior process or results.

nodes.label: low-fragrance-expectation-post-shower
nodes.description: Expects body wash fragrance to last only during the shower, not on skin throughout the day, making scent a nice-to-have rather than a key decision factor.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? Shower_only."
[REAL] "What do you like MOST about your current body wash? Easy to use, cheap, smells okay."
[SYNTHETIC] "Use your regular body wash and layer with a perfume/deo instead."
nodes.predictive_value: A claim of '24-hour fragrance' would be viewed as exaggerated or unnecessary; they'd prioritize other benefits like skin feel or lather.

nodes.label: sample-driven-trialist-before-commitment
nodes.description: Prefers to try a product via a small sample, sachet, or travel size before purchasing a full-size bottle, especially for higher-priced or unfamiliar brands.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size."
[SYNTHETIC] "Pay ₹99 for a trial with credit toward the full size."
[SYNTHETIC] "Ask for a sample first before committing."
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the sachet to try — perfect low-risk trial."
nodes.predictive_value: A 'first-time buyer' offer of a ₹99 trial size with credit toward full purchase would be highly effective in converting them to try a new premium brand.

nodes.label: social-context-fragrance-consciousness
nodes.description: Is consciously aware of body odor and fragrance in social gatherings with close contact, wanting to avoid bothering others, which elevates the importance of effective cleansing and pleasant but not overpowering scent in those situations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Social gatherings with close contact where body odor may bother others."
[REAL] "How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Both."
[SYNTHETIC] "Use a special/premium body wash you reserve for important occasions."
nodes.predictive_value: An ad showing a product used before a wedding, party, or close-group gathering would resonate more than one showing solitary use.

nodes.label: multi-channel-researcher-before-purchase
nodes.description: Even when ready to buy on one platform (e.g., quick commerce), will pause to check reviews and prices on other platforms (Nykaa, Amazon) before finalizing the decision.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first."
[SYNTHETIC] "Search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto."
[SYNTHETIC] "Scan the product in the Nykaa app and read reviews right there before buying."
nodes.predictive_value: A seamless feature within a quick-commerce app that surfaces aggregated ratings and reviews from other platforms would significantly speed up their decision-making.

nodes.label: value-aligned-premium-payer-for-proven-impact
nodes.description: Willing to pay a 20-40% premium for sustainability (eco-packaging, cruelty-free) and 10-20% for clinical-grade formulations, but only if the impact is tangible and proven, not just claimed.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Maximum premium for Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging: 20_40%, Cruelty-free: 20_40%, Clinical-grade: 10_20%."
[SYNTHETIC] "About ₹30-40 — I value it but not that much."
[SYNTHETIC] "I'd need to see the clinical study results first."
nodes.predictive_value: A product claiming 'carbon-neutral' or 'plastic-negative' with third-party certification would justify a price increase, whereas a vague 'eco-friendly' claim would not.

nodes.label: P15_T005
nodes.identity_summary: P15_T005 is a price-driven, pragmatic student who prioritizes cost-effectiveness and verified performance in personal care. They actively research online, heavily weigh expert and friend recommendations, and are willing to pay a limited premium only for proven benefits like clinical efficacy or sustainability. Their purchases are cautious, influenced by credible sources like dermatologists and Reddit, and they readily switch brands for better value or convenience.

nodes.label: price-primary-decision-driver
nodes.description: Consistently ranks price as the top factor when buying personal care, with a typical body wash budget of ₹100-200.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: A: ["price","recommendation","ingredients","brand","expert","fragrance","reviews","packaging"]
[REAL] How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 100_200
[SYNTHETIC] Allocate 100 points across these five factors... A: Price/deal 40, Past experience 30, Skin benefit 15, Brand name 10, Packaging 5.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely first check the price and compare it to their usual spend; a price point above ₹200 without strong justification would be a significant barrier.

nodes.label: recommendation-responsive-expert-and-friend
nodes.description: Ranks recommendations (especially from doctors and friends) as the second most important purchase factor and lists them as top triggers to switch products.

[BRANCH] Ranked purchase triggers: 1) 50% off flash sale 2) Dermatologist's recommendation...
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash ad featuring a dermatologist's endorsement or a 'recommended by friends' social proof claim would significantly increase their likelihood of consideration.

nodes.label: selective-premium-payer-for-proven-values
nodes.description: Willing to pay a 20-40% premium for cruelty-free or sustainable packaging, and 10-20% for clinical-grade formulations, but 0% for Ayurvedic or 'Made in India' claims alone.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Maximum premium (%) you would pay...: {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"20_40","Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"0","Cruelty-free, not tested on animals":"20_40","Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation":"10_20","Made in India":"0"}
[REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health

nodes.predictive_value: A product claiming clinical results with certification or highlighting cruelty-free/sustainable credentials can justify a higher price, but vague 'natural' or 'Indian-made' claims will not.

nodes.label: brand-origin-agnostic

evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? A: No preference
[REAL] Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice...? A: Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same

nodes.predictive_value: Marketing emphasizing 'foreign' or 'swadeshi' origin will not sway them; they will focus on price, ingredients, and performance instead.

nodes.label: pragmatic-brand-switcher-for-function
nodes.description: Switches brands for practical benefits like better packaging, ease of use, or price, not emotional loyalty; satisfied with current brand because it's 'easy to use, cheap, smells okay'.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand... Switched from clumsy option for better packaging and ease of use. Satisfied
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? A: Easy to use, cheap, smells okay

[BRANCH] When given a free sample of a ₹429 body wash vs. regular ₹229, A: I'd go with my regular ₹229... Sampling was nice, but I'm not convinced to spend almost double when my current one works fine
nodes.predictive_value: A new body wash that demonstrates clear functional superiority (e.g., easier dispensing, better lather) or a significant price advantage can easily lure them away from their current brand.

nodes.label: value-seeker-over-loyalist
nodes.description: Prioritizes getting good value for money over sticking with a familiar brand; will switch to a cheaper alternative without much thought if performance seems comparable.

While open to trying new products, they mitigate risk through extensive online research (especially reading mass reviews on platforms like Amazon) and prefer to test via small/trial sizes before committing to full purchases.
