[REAL] Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice... A: Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that emphasizes 'international formula' or 'Swiss technology' would be viewed as a gimmick and not influence this person's purchase decision.

nodes.label: pragmatic-brand-switcher
nodes.description: Switches brands for practical, functional reasons like better packaging and ease of use.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Describe the last time you tried a completely new body wash brand. What triggered the switch? Were you satisfied? A: Switched from clumsy option for better packaging and ease of use. Satisfied
nodes.predictive_value: A new body wash that demonstrates clear functional advantages (like a superior dispenser) would be more likely to trigger a switch than one with only aesthetic or emotional appeals.

nodes.label: occasional-premium-treat-buyer
nodes.description: Will occasionally buy a premium product as a treat while keeping a regular, cheaper option as a backup.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] You walk into a BigBasket... What do you walk out with? A: I might buy both—the ₹429 as a treat since I enjoyed the sample, and my regular as backup.

[SYNTHETIC] You're gifted a premium body wash... After using it, you love it — but it costs 3x your usual brand. What happens next? A: I use it for special occasions only and keep my regular brand for daily use.
nodes.predictive_value: Limited-edition or premium variants can succeed as occasional 'treat' purchases for this person, but they will not replace their daily driver.

nodes.label: social-gathering-scent-conscious
nodes.description: More conscious of body odor and scent during social gatherings with close contact.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When you think about how your scent affects others, is there a particular situation where you're more conscious of it... A: Social gatherings with close contact where body odor may bother others
nodes.predictive_value: Advertising that situates the body wash in a social gathering context (like a party or close group setting) would resonate more than ads focused on solitary moments.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-grooming
nodes.description: The approach to bathing and grooming routines varies depending on their mood.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent
nodes.predictive_value: Product messaging that acknowledges different moods (e.g., 'for days you need a boost' vs. 'for days you need to unwind') would feel more relatable and effective.

nodes.label: context-dependent-ad-engagement
nodes.description: Likelihood of engaging with an ad depends heavily on the immediate context (e.g., studying vs. relaxing).
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] You're studying for an important exam... Later, you're relaxing at home... How does your likelihood of engaging with the ad change... A: It's completely different! When I'm studying, ads are just distractions... At home, I'm in a better mood to explore...
nodes.predictive_value: Ads served during leisure/entertainment moments (e.g., YouTube at night) have a much higher chance of engagement than those served during work/study times.

nodes.label: peer-influenced-by-proximity
nodes.description: Influenced by seeing products in friends' spaces and by direct friend recommendations.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Rank these five purchase triggers... 3) Seeing it in your best friend's bathroom – personal trust adds credibility.
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ["friend_rec"]
nodes.predictive_value: Sampling or visibility strategies that place products in close social circles (e.g., friend gifting, shared apartments) would be an effective conversion trigger.

nodes.label: research-before-buying
nodes.description: Actively researches products by checking reviews and comparing options before making a purchase.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd consider both influencers, but it depends on my current needs... I'd probably check reviews and compare before deciding...
[SYNTHETIC] Allocate 100 points across these five factors... A: Price/deal 50, past experience 30, skin benefit 10, brand 7, packaging 3.
nodes.predictive_value: A new product concept will need to generate substantial and credible third-party reviews (on Amazon, Nykaa) before this person will feel comfortable purchasing.

nodes.label: trusts-unbiased-expert-sources
nodes.description: Prefers and trusts information sources perceived to have no incentive to lie, like journalistic or independent expert content.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? Name specific ones and what makes you trust them. A: Splainer, sjbreddits
[REAL] What is it about their content or approach that makes you trust their recommendations over others? A: Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity
nodes.predictive_value: Content marketing that mimics an unbiased, educational format (e.g., a detailed ingredient explainer video) will be more effective than overtly promotional influencer content.

nodes.label: clinical-claims-and-expert-review-responsive
nodes.description: Pays attention to and is persuaded by claims of clinical studies, dermatologist recommendations, and expert reviews.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ["clinical_studies","derm_recommended","intense_moisture"]
[REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health
nodes.predictive_value: A product page that front-loads clinical study results or a dermatologist seal of approval will capture this person's attention and increase perceived value.

nodes.label: online-reviews-checker
nodes.description: When shopping online, immediately looks at the review count and ratings.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ["price","review_count","brand_name"]

[SYNTHETIC] You're browsing body wash on Nykaa and find two products at ₹349. One has 2,400 ratings with a 4.1 average. The other has 85 ratings with a 4.8 average. Which one do you click on first? A: Click on the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first.
nodes.predictive_value: A new product launch should aim to generate a high volume of reviews quickly, as this person is more likely to trust a product with many reviews over one with a slightly higher but less-populated rating.

nodes.label: moderate-novelty-seeker-with-research
nodes.description: Fairly comfortable trying new brands (4/5) but balances this openness with prior research.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 4
[BRANCH] I'd consider both influencers, but it depends on my current needs... I'd probably check reviews and compare before deciding...
nodes.predictive_value: This person is a viable target for new brand launches, but the brand must provide ample information (reviews, ingredients) to facilitate their research and mitigate perceived risk.

nodes.label: try-before-commit-preference
nodes.description: Prefers to try a product via a sample, mini, or sachet before committing to a full-size purchase.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] You walk into a BigBasket... I might buy both—the ₹429 as a treat since I enjoyed the sample...

[SYNTHETIC] A body wash brand offers two purchase options: (A) Full-size 250ml bottle for ₹349... (B) A 50ml trial for ₹99... Which option do you choose? A: Choose the ₹99 trial with credit toward full size to test before committing.
nodes.predictive_value: Offering low-cost trial sizes or free samples with purchase would significantly lower the barrier for this person to try a new, higher-priced product.

nodes.label: risk-averse-without-proof
nodes.description: Hesitant to pay a significant premium or try unfamiliar ingredients without clinical proof or expert validation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health

[SYNTHETIC] What if your regular ₹299 body wash launched a 'dermatologist-recommended' version at ₹399 with the same ingredients but clinical testing certification? Would you upgrade? A: I'd need to see the clinical study results first.
nodes.predictive_value: Innovative claims (e.g., 'probiotic,' 'bakuchiol') will require clear, credible explanation and validation from trusted sources to overcome initial skepticism.

nodes.label: online-first-shopper
nodes.description: The default action when a product runs out is to browse online, and they frequently shop on Amazon, Nykaa, and local kirana stores.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When your current body wash/soap runs out, what is your typical next step? A: Browse_online
[REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ["amazon","nykaa","kirana"]
nodes.predictive_value: Digital shelf presence (on Amazon, Nykaa) is critical for capturing this person's purchase intent; out-of-stock situations online may lead them to a local store.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-adaptor
nodes.description: Uses quick commerce apps and adapts to their limited selection by buying smaller sizes or acceptable alternatives.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, yes
[REAL] Can you share a recent example where the limited selection on these platforms influenced your choice... A: Bought a smaller skincare unit as a temporary fix; desired product unavailable
nodes.predictive_value: In urgent need scenarios, this person will settle for a familiar or 'good enough' brand available on Blinkit/Zepto, making top listings on these apps valuable for impulse buys.

nodes.label: prefers-minimalist-packaging
nodes.description: Prefers body wash packaging that has a minimalist aesthetic.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts with sleek, simple, and clean packaging designs will have greater visual appeal than overly ornate or colorful packaging.

nodes.label: travel-convenience-seeker
nodes.description: Values functional packaging features like being leakproof for travel.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch, describe it... A: ...leakproof for travel, cheap
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that highlights travel-friendly features (secure cap, compact size) would resonate, especially around holiday or vacation seasons.

nodes.label: lather-essential
nodes.description: Considers a rich lather or foam an essential part of the body wash experience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Essential
[REAL] What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? A: Foams well, does not dry skin
nodes.predictive_value: A product demonstration or claim that emphasizes abundant, luxurious lather will be a strong positive signal, especially for products targeting hard water conditions.

nodes.label: lightweight-texture-preference
nodes.description: Prefers a lightweight texture in a body wash over thick or creamy consistencies.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight
nodes.predictive_value: When comparing products online, descriptions like 'lightweight gel' or 'watery texture' will be more appealing than 'rich cream' or 'buttery' textures.

nodes.label: fragrance-preference-aquatic-gourmand
nodes.description: Naturally gravitates towards ocean/aquatic and sweet gourmand fragrance families.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: A: Ocean_aquatic,sweet_gourmand
nodes.predictive_value: New fragrance variants described as 'ocean breeze,' 'sea salt,' or 'vanilla caramel' would have a higher chance of appealing to this person's scent preferences.

nodes.label: fragrance-duration-realistic
nodes.description: Expects body wash fragrance to last only during the shower, not on skin throughout the day.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Shower_only
nodes.predictive_value: Claims of '24-hour fragrance' or 'long-lasting scent' would be viewed with skepticism; honest claims about a pleasant in-shower experience would be more credible.

nodes.label: post-wash-neutral-skin-feel
nodes.description: Prefers a body wash that leaves the skin feeling neutral—not too dry or too slippery/moist.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Neutral
nodes.predictive_value: Products marketed as 'non-drying' and 'rinse clean without residue' would align perfectly with this person's desired outcome.

nodes.label: skin-benefit-focused-on-dryness
nodes.description: Has skin concern of dryness and seeks body washes that address it by not drying out skin while foaming well.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? Select all that apply. A: Dryness
[REAL] What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? A: Foams well, does not dry skin
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash concept explicitly formulated for 'dry skin' or 'intense moisture' that also promises good lather would immediately capture their interest.

nodes.label: P15_T010
nodes.identity_summary: This consumer is a value-driven, research-oriented student who prioritizes price and proven efficacy in body wash purchases. They are highly responsive to expert recommendations (especially dermatologists) and clinical claims, but also actively seek discounts and rely on multi-platform reviews before buying. Their decisions are cautious and practical, balancing a willingness to pay for scientifically-backed benefits with a strong aversion to unnecessary premium for packaging, origin, or vague marketing claims.

nodes.label: price-primary-decision-driver
nodes.description: Consistently ranks price as the #1 factor when buying personal care products and bases final purchase decisions heavily on the immediate deal available.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: A: ["price","recommendation","ingredients","brand","expert","fragrance","reviews","packaging"]
[REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ["doctor_rec","friend_rec","cheaper"]

[SYNTHETIC] Allocate 100 points across these five factors based on what actually drives your final body wash purchase decision... A: Price/deal 40, Past experience 30, Skin benefit 15, Brand recognition 10, Packaging 5.
[SYNTHETIC] If two body washes are identical, I buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249 over the sleek minimalist one at ₹399.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new product concept, this person will first anchor on the price point and is highly likely to engage with messaging highlighting a clear discount or superior value-for-money.

nodes.label: expert-recommendation-trumps-popularity
nodes.description: Deeply trusts recommendations from credentialed experts (dermatologists, skincare scientists) over those from high-follower influencers, family, or general social proof.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? ... Splainer, sjbreddits. What makes you trust them? Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity.

[BRANCH] I'd be more influenced by Influencer A (skincare expert, 50K followers) recommends a ₹449 clinical body wash. Influencer B (lifestyle creator, 2M followers) recommends a ₹249 fragrant body wash.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd trust the dermatologist since it's their field over my aunt with a biology background.
[SYNTHETIC] A dermatologist campaign makes me more likely to buy than a Bollywood celebrity campaign.
nodes.predictive_value: Ads or concepts featuring dermatologist endorsements or clinical study data will capture their attention and significantly increase purchase intent, even at a higher price point.

nodes.label: clinical-proof-premium-justifier
nodes.description: Explicitly willing to pay a significant premium (up to 2x) only for benefits backed by rigorous, visible scientific validation like clinical studies.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health.
[REAL] What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: ["clinical_studies","derm_recommended","intense_moisture"].
[SYNTHETIC] For a 'dermatologist-recommended' version at ₹399 vs regular ₹299 with same ingredients: I'd need to see the clinical study results first.
[REAL] Maximum premium for 'Clinical-grade, lab-tested formulation': 10_20%.
nodes.predictive_value: A product claiming 'clinically tested' without accessible study details will be met with skepticism; they require transparent proof to justify any price increase.

nodes.label: multi-platform-review-cross-checker
nodes.description: Does not trust reviews on a single platform; actively cross-references ratings and feedback across Amazon, Nykaa, Reddit, and YouTube before purchasing.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] If reviews on Nykaa seem incentivized, I check another platform for more authentic reviews.
[SYNTHETIC] On Zepto, if I see a 'Bestseller' tag but no reviews, I search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering.
[SYNTHETIC] If a brand website says suitable for sensitive skin but Amazon reviews mention irritation, I search for more reviews on other platforms.
[REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: ["price","review_count","brand_name"].
nodes.predictive_value: A product with strong but isolated reviews on one platform will be less effective than one with consistent, verifiable praise across multiple independent sites.

nodes.label: brand-origin-agnostic
nodes.description: Views country of origin as a marketing gimmick, believing production processes are largely the same, and thus shows no inherent bias for Indian or international brands.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How do you feel about Indian personal care brands vs. international brands? Do you have a preference or bias? A: No preference.
[REAL] Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice...? A: Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd buy the ₹249 'Made in India' option over identical 'Imported from Korea' at ₹449; origin doesn't matter but price does.
[REAL] Maximum premium for 'Made in India': 0%.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing leaning heavily on 'foreign' or 'swadeshi' origin will be ineffective; they will evaluate purely on formulation, price, and proof points.

nodes.label: discount-triggered-experimenter
nodes.description: While generally loyal to known products, a strong discount (e.g., 50% off) is a primary trigger to try a completely new brand.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Rank these five purchase triggers from most to least likely to make you buy a body wash you haven't tried before: 1) 50% off flash sale on Nykaa.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd be more likely to act on an ad from a competitor offering ₹75 off than an ad from my current brand with just beautiful visuals.
[REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands or products you haven't used before? A: 4 (on a likely 5-point scale).
[SYNTHETIC] If my regular body wash increased price by ₹80, I'd try a friend-recommended D2C brand at the old price.
nodes.predictive_value: Limited-time flash sales and strong first-purchase discounts are highly effective tactics to drive trial and break their usual routine.

nodes.label: sample-and-trial-before-commitment
nodes.description: Prefers to mitigate the risk of trying new products through small, low-cost formats like sachets, travel minis, or free samples before buying full-size.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd rank free sample sachets as a top purchase trigger for new products.
[SYNTHETIC] After a bad experience with a new body wash, I'd only try another new brand if they offer a 50ml trial size.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd buy a ₹10 sachet at a kirana store to try a ₹399 body wash — perfect low-risk trial.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd choose a ₹99 trial size with credit toward full size over committing to a full ₹349 bottle upfront.
nodes.predictive_value: Offering affordable trial sizes or free samples will lower their barrier to entry and increase conversion to full-size purchases for new products.

nodes.label: sustainable-values-with-capped-premium
nodes.description: Willing to pay a moderate premium (20-40%) for tangible ethical values like cruelty-free and sustainable packaging, but not for vague 'natural' or Ayurvedic claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? ... {"Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging":"20_40","Ayurvedic or traditional ingredients":"0","Cruelty-free, not tested on animals":"20_40".}
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? ... A: Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free.
[SYNTHETIC] For a ₹349 body wash with recyclable packaging & donation vs ₹269 identical one: I'd pay ₹30-40 extra, not all ₹80.
[REAL] How important is it that a product is 'natural'? Rating: 3. Explanation: Irrelevant unless a known harmful effect exists.
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing highlighting cruelty-free certification or eco-packaging can justify a modest price increase, but 'natural' or 'Ayurvedic' claims alone will not.

nodes.label: functional-fragrance-expectation
nodes.description: Prefers simple, safe, long-lasting scents (oceanic/sweet) but expects fragrance to be a shower-only experience, not lasting on skin post-rinse.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Shower_only.
[REAL] Name a specific product whose fragrance you loved... Titan Skinn Verge. Simple, safe scent; long-lasting.
[REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: Ocean_aquatic,sweet_gourmand.
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? ... smells okay.
nodes.predictive_value: Product concepts promising '24-hour fragrance' will be viewed as unnecessary or misleading; they will respond better to claims about in-shower scent experience and skin-compatible fragrances.

nodes.label: lather-and-lightweight-texture-essential
nodes.description: Considers rich lather/foam a non-negotiable attribute and prefers a lightweight, watery texture that doesn't leave a heavy or dry feel.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Essential.
[REAL] What texture do you prefer in a body wash? A: Lightweight.
[REAL] What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Neutral.
[REAL] Describe your PERFECT body wash: Watery texture, foams directly via dispenser, non-drying but effective clean.
nodes.predictive_value: Ads or demos visually emphasizing copious, luxurious lather and a light, fast-absorbing texture will strongly resonate with their definition of an effective clean.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-for-immediacy-over-selection
nodes.description: Uses quick commerce (Blinkit/Zepto) for urgent needs despite limited selection and higher prices, valuing speed over optimal choice when stock is low.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you use quick commerce for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, yes.
[SYNTHETIC] I'm almost out of body wash: I order from Blinkit at full price for instant delivery over waiting 5 days for a discounted Amazon order.
[SYNTHETIC] In a rush on Zepto with my usual brand unavailable, I pick the cheapest temporary substitute.
[REAL] Can you share a recent example where the limited selection on these platforms influenced your choice...? A: Bought a smaller skincare unit as a temporary fix; desired product unavailable.
nodes.predictive_value: This person is a prime target for 'top-up' campaigns on quick commerce apps, especially for staple products, but will not explore new variants deeply on these platforms.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-skeptic-verifier
nodes.description: Actively reads ingredient lists to verify specific claims (e.g., 'Paraben-Free') and recognizes common key ingredients like SLS, but doesn't deeply analyze every component.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] If I see 'Paraben-Free' on a bottle, I flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list.
[SYNTHETIC] I recognize a few key ingredients like SLS when reading a label.
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? ... sulfate_free,paraben_free.
[SYNTHETIC] Before buying, I allocate 25 out of 100 research points to 'Checking the ingredient list'.
nodes.predictive_value: Product pages that provide a clear, legible ingredient list and highlight the absence of specific flagged ingredients (SLS, parabens) will satisfy their verification habit.

nodes.label: reddit-community-trust-over-influencer
nodes.description: Places high trust in aggregated, anonymous user experiences and discussions on platforms like Reddit over polished influencer content, valuing perceived lack of commercial bias.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media? ... sjbreddits (Reddit). What makes you trust them? Lack of incentive to lie.
[SYNTHETIC] A Reddit thread with before/after photos influences me more than a brand's clinical trial claim.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd try a body wash recommended on r/IndianSkincareAddicts over one promoted by a Nykaa influencer in a paid partnership.
[REAL] Have you ever bought a personal care product because you saw it on social media? ... Yes, on Reddit. Multiple positive comments.
nodes.predictive_value: Seeding products in authentic, review-focused online communities (like Reddit) will generate more credible and influential word-of-mouth than standard influencer gifting.

nodes.label: aspirational-ad-responsive-in-social-context
nodes.description: Drawn to aspirational advertising (e.g., celebrity, luxury settings) specifically because it aligns with their desire to feel special and make a good impression in social gatherings.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] I'd be drawn to Ad A with the celebrity at the luxury party. It makes the product feel special and desirable, which appeals to me for social gatherings where I want to smell good and make an impression.
[REAL] When you think about how your scent affects others, is there a particular situation where you're more conscious of it...? A: Social gatherings with close contact.
[REAL] How much do you care about how you smell to others...? A: Both (feeling good yourself and others' perception).
nodes.predictive_value: Ad creative that visually ties the product to aspirational social success or confident self-presentation in gatherings will be more engaging than purely functional or comedic ads.

nodes.label: minimalist-packaging-preference
nodes.description: Prefers packaging that is minimalist in design, but is unwilling to pay a significant premium for aesthetics alone.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist.
[SYNTHETIC] I would not pay an extra ₹250 for customized packaging with my name on it.
[SYNTHETIC] I'd skip a limited edition with designer packaging costing ₹150 more for the same formula.
[REAL] Rank of 'packaging' in purchase factors: Last (8th).
nodes.predictive_value: While sleek, minimalist bottle design is appreciated, product concepts should not hinge on or charge extra for elaborate packaging, as it's a low-priority factor.

nodes.label: hard-water-performance-seeker
nodes.description: Specifically interested in body washes formulated for Indian skin and hard water conditions, with key performance indicators being good lather and non-drying effect.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? Rating: 4.
[REAL] What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? A: Foams well, does not dry skin.
[REAL] Skin concerns: Dryness.
nodes.predictive_value: New product concepts that explicitly address hard water lathering and dryness prevention will capture their attention as solving a relevant, practical problem.

nodes.label: practical-refill-aversion
nodes.description: Views refill packs as not economical and prefers self-contained packaging, showing reluctance toward refill systems that require separate dispensers.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What, if anything, frustrates you or feels 'just okay' about your current body wash? A: Refill packs are not economical.
[SYNTHETIC] If a body wash came in a refill pouch needing a reusable dispenser I don't own, I'd buy the standard plastic bottle.
[SYNTHETIC] I would not invest ₹200 in a reusable glass bottle system even with cheaper refills.
nodes.predictive_value: Promoting refill pouches as a cheaper or greener option may backfire; they need clear, unambiguous cost-per-ml savings and extreme convenience to consider refills.

nodes.label: mood-dependent-grooming-routine
nodes.description: Approach to bathing and grooming is not rigidly ritualistic but varies depending on their mood, indicating a flexible, non-obsessive relationship with personal care.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How would you describe your relationship with bathing/grooming? A: Mood_dependent.
nodes.predictive_value: Products positioned as 'daily essentials' or 'rituals' should maintain a tone of flexible enhancement rather than rigid necessity to align with their casual approach.

nodes.label: value-per-ml-calculator
nodes.description: Makes purchase decisions based on calculated value per ml, opting for larger sizes when the per-unit price is better, even if the upfront cost is higher.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] If my regular body wash became available only in a 500ml size at a slightly cheaper per-ml price, I'd buy the larger size — better value per ml.
[REAL] The most I have ever paid for a body wash (~₹200) was worth it because it lasted long.
[SYNTHETIC] On Zepto, if I only need body wash for two weeks, I pick the 100ml travel size over the 250ml, showing situational value calculation.
nodes.predictive_value: Clearly displaying per-ml or per-use cost comparisons (e.g., '40% more product for only 20% more cost') will be an effective persuasion tool.

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-weighed-but-researched
nodes.description: Values recommendations from friends but does not adopt them blindly; uses them as a signal to begin independent research rather than an immediate trigger to buy.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] If a friend recommends a body wash, I'd look up the brand and see if it suits my skin type too.
[SYNTHETIC] In a WhatsApp group with 8 friend recommendations, I'd research each suggestion independently.
[REAL] What would make you switch? Top 3 includes 'friend_rec'.
[BRANCH] Rank of 'seeing it in your best friend's bathroom' as a purchase trigger: Last (5th).
nodes.predictive_value: Friend referral programs may initiate their curiosity, but the product must then have strong independent reviews and clear benefits to convert them.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-traditional-claims-skeptic
nodes.description: Assigns zero premium to 'Ayurvedic' or 'traditional ingredient' claims, viewing them as irrelevant unless backed by proven, tangible effects.

[REAL] What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? A: Foams well, does not dry skin
nodes.predictive_value: Body washes that promise intense moisture or are formulated for dry skin will be particularly appealing.

nodes.label: fragrance-preference-aquatic-gourmand
nodes.description: Naturally gravitates toward ocean/aquatic and sweet gourmand fragrance families.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Pick your top 2: Ocean_aquatic,sweet_gourmand
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash with fresh aquatic or sweet dessert-like scents would be more likely to attract this person.

nodes.label: fragrance-longevity-low-expectation
nodes.description: Expects body wash fragrance to last only during the shower, not throughout the day.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Shower_only
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that emphasizes all-day fragrance would not be a compelling claim for this person.

nodes.label: willing-to-pay-for-clinical-proof
nodes.description: Willing to pay up to twice the current price for clinically proven positive effects on skin health.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health
nodes.predictive_value: A body wash backed by strong clinical evidence and dermatologist recommendations can command a higher price from this person.

nodes.label: discount-responsive
nodes.description: Highly responsive to discounts, flash sales, and cashback offers, often ranking them as top purchase triggers.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What type of content makes you most likely to try a new product? Pick top 2: Discount,expert_review
[BRANCH] I'd rank them: 1) 50% off flash sale – saving money is always a smart move, especially as a student.
nodes.predictive_value: Limited-time discounts or bundle offers will significantly increase purchase likelihood.

nodes.label: social-media-influenced-with-caution
nodes.description: Has bought products based on social media (Reddit) but values multiple positive comments and independent research.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Have you ever bought a personal care product because you saw it on social media? Describe what happened. A: Yes, performed as expected
[REAL] Which social media platform... A: Reddit. Multiple positive comments
nodes.predictive_value: Social media ads with many genuine positive comments (e.g., Reddit threads) can drive trial, but the person will still verify.

nodes.label: relatable-advertising-preference
nodes.description: Prefers advertising that features realistic, relatable scenarios (e.g., student in hostel) over aspirational celebrity-driven ads.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q63: I'd engage more with Ad B showing the student in the hostel bathroom. It feels genuine and relevant to my daily life as a student in Ahmedabad...
nodes.predictive_value: Ads that depict everyday situations and practical benefits are more likely to engage this person than luxury-focused ads.

nodes.label: treat-and-backup-purchasing
nodes.description: Sometimes buys both a premium product as a treat and a regular product as a backup, balancing indulgence and practicality.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q62: I might buy both—the ₹429 as a treat since I enjoyed the sample, and my regular as backup.
nodes.predictive_value: Sampling programs that allow trial of premium products can lead to occasional splurges, but the person will maintain a practical backup.

nodes.label: ingredient-awareness-without-obsession
nodes.description: Pays attention to ingredients, especially harmful ones, but is not overly concerned with 'natural' claims unless there is a known harmful effect.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? A: rating 3, explanation: Locally sourced, fresh and homemade
[REAL] How do these qualities influence your decision when choosing beauty products if at all? A: Irrelevant unless a known harmful effect exists
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that highlights the absence of specific harmful ingredients (sulfates, parabens) is more effective than vague 'natural' claims.

nodes.label: certification-influenced
nodes.description: Certifications like cruelty-free, dermatologist-tested, sulfate-free, and paraben-free influence purchase decisions.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free
nodes.predictive_value: Displaying relevant certifications on packaging or in ads can positively influence this person's choice.

nodes.label: prefers-expert-over-lifestyle-influencer
nodes.description: More influenced by skincare experts with detailed ingredient analysis than lifestyle creators with large followings.
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q64: I'd be more influenced by Influencer A. As someone who trusts skincare expertise...
[SYNTHETIC] Q119: Influencer A, the skincare expert with detailed analysis.
nodes.predictive_value: Collaborations with dermatologists or expert reviewers will be more effective than partnerships with general lifestyle influencers.

nodes.label: context-dependent-ad-engagement
nodes.description: More likely to engage with ads when relaxed (e.g., at home after dinner) than when busy (studying for exams).
evidence_quotes: [BRANCH] Q65: I'm more likely to engage when I'm relaxed at home—I can properly watch and consider the ad.
nodes.predictive_value: Timing ads for evening relaxation periods or weekends may yield higher engagement from this person.

nodes.label: reviews-over-rating
nodes.description: Values the number of reviews and detailed text reviews over a high average rating with few reviews.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q93: Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? A: Brand A
[SYNTHETIC] Q166: A) Brand A — more reviews mean more reliable ratings.
nodes.predictive_value: A product with many reviews (even with a slightly lower rating) will be perceived as more trustworthy than one with a high rating but few reviews.

nodes.label: refill-pack-economy-conscious
nodes.description: Frustrated when refill packs are not economical compared to original packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What, if anything, frustrates you or feels 'just okay' about your current body wash? A: Refill packs are not economical
nodes.predictive_value: Refill options that offer clear cost savings will be appreciated; otherwise, they may stick to original packaging.

nodes.label: travel-size-for-convenience
nodes.description: Willing to buy travel-size or sachets when traveling or for temporary needs, despite higher per-unit cost.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Q74: Buy sachet when traveling and need something small. Convenient size justifies cost.
[SYNTHETIC] Q312: Pick the 100ml travel size for two weeks.
nodes.predictive_value: Travel-sized products and single-use sachets are considered for specific situations like trips or trials.

nodes.label: price-first-buyer-in-final-decision-moment
nodes.description: When ready to purchase, price and immediate deals become the dominant factor, outweighing brand, packaging, and even some skin benefits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "price" is ranked first in importance when buying personal care products (Q11).
[SYNTHETIC] "Allocate 100 points... Price/deal 50, Past experience 30, Skin benefit 10, Brand name 7, Packaging 3." (Q79)
[SYNTHETIC] "Switch to the ₹249 alternative without much thought." (Q72)
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product concept with a premium price point without a clear, immediate discount or superior value proof, this person would likely reject it in favor of a cheaper known alternative.

nodes.label: pragmatic-openness-to-trial-via-minimal-risk-channels
nodes.description: Willing to try new products but prefers low-commitment methods like small/travel sizes, samples, or strong money-back guarantees before full purchase.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Comfort level with trying new brands is 4 out of 5 (Q7).
[SYNTHETIC] "Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size." (Q211)
[SYNTHETIC] "Choose the 50ml trial for ₹99 with credit toward full size to test before committing." (Q216)
[SYNTHETIC] "Yes — the guarantee removes my risk, I'll try it." (Q92)
nodes.predictive_value: When launching a new variant, offering a ₹99 mini or a "try-before-you-commit" sample will dramatically increase their likelihood of trial versus asking for a full-price purchase upfront.

nodes.label: expert-credibility-seeker-over-influencer-hype
nodes.description: Prioritizes and trusts information from perceived impartial experts (dermatologists, journalistic reviewers) over mainstream influencers or celebrity endorsements.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Trusts sources like "Splainer, sjbreddits" due to "Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity." (Q56, Q57)
[REAL] Top claims that make them stop and read: "clinical_studies", "derm_recommended" (Q50).
[BRANCH] Ranks "Dermatologist's recommendation" as a top-2 purchase trigger (Q61).
[SYNTHETIC] "Whose recommendation carries more weight? ...Friend B — I trust their thoroughness and knowledge." (Q200)
nodes.predictive_value: An ad featuring a dermatologist explaining pH balance or clinical results will be far more effective than one featuring a celebrity or a lifestyle influencer.

nodes.label: functional-fragrance-preference-shower-only-longevity
nodes.description: Views fragrance as a pleasant but non-essential shower experience; explicitly does not want or expect it to last on skin post-rinse.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? Shower_only" (Q36).
[REAL] Likes Titan Skinn Verge for being a "Simple, safe scent" (Q35).
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that emphasizes "24-hour fragrance" or "all-day scent trail" will be ignored or viewed as irrelevant. Focus should be on the in-shower sensory experience.

nodes.label: lather-essentialist
nodes.description: Considers rich lather/foam a non-negotiable, essential attribute of a satisfactory body wash experience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? Essential" (Q37).
[REAL] For a body wash for Indian hard water, a key benefit is "Foams well" (Q53).
nodes.predictive_value: A product that fails to produce abundant lather, especially in hard water, will be immediately rejected regardless of other benefits.

nodes.label: solution-seeker-for-specific-skin-concern-dryness
nodes.description: Actively notices skin problems (dryness) and is motivated by products that offer tangible, proven solutions for this specific issue.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "What specific skin concerns, if any, do you have? ... Dryness" (Q33).
[REAL] "How much attention do you pay to how your skin looks and feels on a daily basis? Notice_problems" (Q10).
[REAL] Would pay 2x price for "Clinically proven positive effects on skin health" (Q54).
nodes.predictive_value: Claims around "intense moisture," "non-drying formula," or "clinically proven to improve skin hydration" will be highly salient and can justify a price premium.

nodes.label: value-maximizer-not-minimizer
nodes.description: Seeks the best combination of price and perceived quality/benefits within a mid-range budget, rather than automatically choosing the absolute cheapest option.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Typical spend is ₹100-200 (Q21).
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy two mid-range body washes at ₹250 each to cover the full 3 months." (Q68)
[SYNTHETIC] "Rank: B) ₹299 mid-tier, A) ₹149 basic, C) ₹499 organic D2C." (Q86)
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the larger 500ml size for better value per ml." (Q83)
nodes.predictive_value: Will respond well to bulk discounts, combo offers, or larger pack sizes that improve per-use value, but will reject premium products where the value isn't clearly justified.

nodes.label: practicality-and-ease-of-use-driver
nodes.description: Chooses products and formats (like body wash over soap) primarily for practical advantages like convenience, ease of use, and better packaging.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Uses "Body wash due to ease of use" (Q31).
[REAL] Last switch was triggered by desire for "better packaging and ease of use" (Q23).
[REAL] Ideal body wash includes a dispenser that "foams directly" and is "leakproof for travel" (Q49).
nodes.predictive_value: Innovations in packaging (foam pumps, travel locks) and format (easy-to-dispense) will be strong purchase drivers, sometimes even over fragrance or ingredient claims.

nodes.label: ingredient-aware-but-not-dogmatic
nodes.description: Reads labels and is aware of certain ingredients (SLS, parabens) but is not driven by 'clean beauty' hype; only avoids ingredients with known harmful effects.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "Irrelevant unless a known harmful effect exists" regarding natural/chemical-free qualities (Q14).
[REAL] Checks for certifications like "sulfate_free,paraben_free" (Q15).
[SYNTHETIC] "Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list." (Q109)
[SYNTHETIC] "Research more deeply before deciding." (Q130)
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing focused solely on "chemical-free" or "100% natural" without proof of efficacy or harm reduction will be less effective than highlighting the functional benefit of key ingredients.

nodes.label: brand-origin-agnostic
nodes.description: Believes brand origin (Indian vs. International) is a marketing gimmick and shows zero willingness to pay a premium for it, focusing solely on product efficacy.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "No preference" between Indian and international brands (Q16).
[REAL] "Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same" (Q17).
[REAL] Willingness to pay premium for "Made in India": "0" (Q18).
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the ₹249 'Made in India' product since origin doesn't influence me." (Q84)
nodes.predictive_value: Patriotic or "support local" marketing will not resonate. Messaging must center on universal product benefits, not origin.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-user-for-urgent-replenishment
nodes.description: Relies on quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto) for immediate needs, accepting limited selection and sometimes higher prices for instant gratification.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Uses quick commerce for personal care: "Yes, yes" (limited selection changes purchase) (Q25).
[SYNTHETIC] "Order on Blinkit for 10-minute delivery since I'm almost out and need it now." (Q69)
[SYNTHETIC] "Pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery when needed urgently." (Q330)
nodes.predictive_value: For top-of-mind awareness and last-minute purchases, being stocked and prominent on quick-commerce apps is critical.

nodes.label: cross-platform-review-checker
nodes.description: Doesn't trust ratings on a single platform; will pause a purchase to check reviews on multiple sites (e.g., Nykaa, Amazon) for validation.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first." (Q113)
[SYNTHETIC] "Check another platform for more authentic reviews." (Q118)
[SYNTHETIC] "Search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto." (Q125)
nodes.predictive_value: A product with high ratings on only one platform (e.g., only on the brand's website) will face skepticism until reviews proliferate on major marketplaces.

nodes.label: sustainability-premium-capped-at-moderate-level
nodes.description: Values sustainable/eco-friendly and cruelty-free attributes and is willing to pay a 20-40% premium, but not an unlimited one.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Willingness to pay premium: "Sustainable/eco-friendly packaging": "20_40", "Cruelty-free, not tested on animals": "20_40" (Q18).
[SYNTHETIC] "About ₹30-40 — I value it but not that much." (Q101)
nodes.predictive_value: A product with strong sustainability credentials priced within a 30% premium of a standard alternative will be considered; beyond that, the value perception drops sharply.

nodes.label: social-proof-reliant-on-crowd-not-curation
nodes.description: Trusts aggregate review counts and ratings from large numbers of users over curated "bestseller" tags or small-sample high ratings.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Trust Brand A more because of the larger review count and better social proof." (Q93)
[SYNTHETIC] "Click on the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first." (Q105)
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy from Nykaa for more reviews and higher rating despite the ₹40 difference." (Q339)
nodes.predictive_value: Displaying high review counts (e.g., "10,000+ reviews") is more persuasive than a "Editor's Pick" or "Nykaa Luxe" badge.

nodes.label: low-tolerance-for-product-failure-switches-back-immediately
nodes.description: If a new product causes issues (like dryness), will immediately discontinue use and revert to a trusted option without trying to make it work.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Stop using it immediately and go back to your old body wash." (Q205)
[SYNTHETIC] Inferred from low risk tolerance and reliance on trusted products.
nodes.predictive_value: A failed product trial doesn't lead to experimentation with adjustments; it leads to immediate abandonment and potential long-term aversion to the brand.

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-weighed-but-researched
nodes.description: Values recommendations from friends but will independently verify with their own research (reviews, ingredients) before acting on them.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] "friend_rec" is a top-3 reason to switch (Q22).
[SYNTHETIC] "Look up both brands online before deciding." (Q200)
[SYNTHETIC] "Choose my close friend's recommendation." (Q106) but context suggests prior trust.
nodes.predictive_value: A friend's recommendation opens the door for consideration but is not a blind trigger for purchase; the product must still pass their personal research criteria.

nodes.label: preference-for-deep-dive-technical-content
nodes.description: When consuming review content, prefers detailed, technical analyses (ingredient breakdowns, comparisons) over short, superficial overviews.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] "Choose the detailed ingredient breakdown with percentages." (Q111)
[SYNTHETIC] "Watch the 15-minute deep dive covering ingredients and comparisons." (Q131)
[SYNTHETIC] "Skincare expert with detailed ingredient analysis." (Q119)
nodes.predictive_value: Long-form blog posts or video reviews that dissect formulations and compare to competitors will be more engaging and persuasive than quick TikTok-style reviews.

nodes.label: channel-agnostic-for-best-deal-post-research
nodes.description: After deciding on a product, will buy from whichever channel (Amazon, Nykaa, Kirana) offers the best combination of price and convenience at that moment.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Top purchase channels: amazon, nykaa, kirana (Q20).
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy from Amazon for free Prime delivery and convenience." (Q334)
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy from kirana store for immediate availability and to support local shop." (Q324)
nodes.predictive_value: Brands need to ensure price parity and availability across major online and offline channels to capture this consumer at the point of decision.

nodes.label: indifferent-to-aesthetic-packaging-premiums
nodes.description: Prefers minimalist packaging but is completely unwilling to pay extra for it; views packaging as functional, not a value-add.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Packaging preference: "Minimalist" but it's the least important factor in purchase (Q11, Q40).
[SYNTHETIC] "Buy the basic plastic bottle at ₹249 since price is more important than packaging." (Q73)
[SYNTHETIC] "Skip it — I'm paying for the formula, not the packaging." (Q161)
nodes.predictive_value: Investing in ultra-premium packaging to justify a higher price will backfire. Packaging should be functional, durable, and cost-effective.

nodes.label: P15_T016
nodes.identity_summary: P15_T016 is a value-conscious, independent student shopper who approaches body wash as a functional daily essential rather than a luxury. They are deeply research-oriented and skeptical of marketing claims, prioritizing dermatologist validation and clinical proof over influencer hype or brand origin. While budget-driven and preferring lightweight, minimalist products, they will pay a premium (20-40%) for cruelty-free products, sustainable packaging, and formulations with clinically proven skin health benefits.

nodes.label: dermatologist-validation-seeker
nodes.description: Prioritizes recommendations and endorsements from medical experts (dermatologists) over any other source, viewing them as the highest form of credibility.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What claims or product descriptions on a body wash would make you stop and read more? Pick top 3: A: ['clinical_studies','derm_recommended','intense_moisture']
[BRANCH] Q: Rank these five purchase triggers... A: I'd say: 1) Dermatologist's recommendation – expert validation matters for skin health.
[SYNTHETIC] Q: Your roommate brings home a Korean body wash... Would you: ... B) Try it only after Googling the brand and ingredients.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown an ad or product page featuring a dermatologist's endorsement or clinical study data, they are significantly more likely to engage and consider purchase.

nodes.label: price-value-optimizer-not-minimizer
nodes.description: Seeks the best value within a defined budget range (₹100-₹200), willing to pay more for proven benefits or convenience, but defaults to cost-saving behaviors when value isn't clear.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: How much do you typically spend on a single body wash purchase? A: 100_200
[REAL] Q: What would a body wash need to offer for you to pay 2x your current price? A: Clinically proven positive effects on skin health

[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're comparing body washes online... Brand A: 4.2 stars, 15,000 reviews, ₹279. Brand B: 4.8 stars, 120 reviews, ₹329. Which do you trust more and buy? A: Brand A with 4.2 stars and 15,000 reviews at ₹279.
nodes.predictive_value: When presented with a price increase, they will switch to a cheaper alternative unless the premium is justified by a specific, proven benefit they value.

nodes.label: function-over-fragrance-focus
nodes.description: Views body wash primarily as a cleansing tool; fragrance is a nice-to-have but secondary to efficacy, skin feel, and lather. Prefers scent to be subtle and not linger.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? A: Shower_only
[REAL] Q: How important is lather/foam to your body wash experience? A: Essential
[REAL] Q: What is your ideal post-wash skin feeling? A: Neutral
[SYNTHETIC] Q: Imagine a body wash that's intentionally unscented... would you: ... B) Try it but feel like something is missing in your shower experience.
nodes.predictive_value: When evaluating a new concept, they will prioritize claims about cleansing efficacy, lather quality, and skin compatibility over elaborate fragrance stories.

nodes.label: skeptical-of-marketing-hype
nodes.description: Actively distrusts marketing claims (like 'natural,' brand origin, celebrity endorsement) and seeks independent verification through reviews, ingredient lists, and expert opinion.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Is there a particular reason why the origin of a brand doesn't influence your choice... A: Brand origin is a marketing gimmick; production processes are usually the same
[REAL] Q: Who do you follow or trust in the personal care space on social media?... A: Splainer, sjbreddits... Lack of incentive to lie, journalistic integrity
[SYNTHETIC] Q: A new D2C body wash brand offers you a free product if you post an honest review... What do you do? A: Post an honest, mixed review as promised.
nodes.predictive_value: When exposed to aspirational or emotionally driven advertising, they will be dismissive and more likely to seek out objective, third-party validation before considering the product.

nodes.label: cross-platform-researcher-before-purchase
nodes.description: Routinely checks multiple platforms (Amazon, Nykaa, Reddit, Google) for reviews and ingredient details before buying, even when shopping on quick commerce.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? Pick your top 3: A: ['price','review_count','brand_name']
[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're on Blinkit ordering essentials and need body wash urgently... Do you: ... B) pause and open Nykaa/Amazon to read reviews first
[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're browsing body wash on Nykaa and find two products at ₹349... Which one do you click on first? A: Click on the one with 2,400 ratings and 4.1 average first.
nodes.predictive_value: A product page with sparse reviews or lacking detailed ingredient information will trigger them to leave the site to research elsewhere, increasing drop-off risk.

nodes.label: ingredient-list-scanner
nodes.description: Scans ingredient lists for key items they recognize (SLS, parabens) and looks for certifications (cruelty-free, dermatologist-tested) as a credibility filter.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Select all that apply. A: Cruelty_free,dermatologist_tested,sulfate_free,paraben_free
[SYNTHETIC] Q: You notice your body wash has 'Paraben-Free' printed on it. What would you most likely do? A: Flip the bottle to verify in the ingredient list
[SYNTHETIC] Q: What if a body wash ingredient — Sodium Lauryl Sulfate — is considered harsh... Would you: ... D) continue using it since it hasn't caused issues
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a product, they will immediately look for and process certification badges and scan the ingredient list for red flags before reading marketing copy.

nodes.label: cruelty-free-premium-payer
nodes.description: Explicitly willing to pay a 20-40% premium for products that are cruelty-free, indicating this is a core, non-negotiable value.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? Rate each separately: A: {'Cruelty-free, not tested on animals':'20_40'}
[SYNTHETIC] Q: Imagine you discover that a body wash brand you love tests on animals... What do you do? A: B) Feel conflicted but continue buying while looking for alternatives.
nodes.predictive_value: When choosing between two similar products, the one with a legitimate cruelty-free certification will win, even at a higher price point.

nodes.label: convenience-driven-channel-switcher
nodes.description: Selects purchase channels based on immediate need, balancing speed (quick commerce), price (online marketplaces), and availability, often using multiple channels.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Where do you most often buy personal care products? Pick your top 3: A: ['amazon','nykaa','kirana']
[REAL] Q: Do you use quick commerce (Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart) for personal care? If yes, does the limited selection change what you buy? A: Yes, yes
[SYNTHETIC] Q: You're about to run out of body wash tonight. You can either order from Blinkit... or order from Amazon... Which do you choose? A: Order from Amazon and get preferred brand tomorrow.
nodes.predictive_value: They are a multi-channel shopper unlikely to be loyal to a single retailer; availability of their preferred product at the right price/pace dictates where they buy.

nodes.label: low-friction-packaging-preferrer
nodes.description: Prefers minimalist, functional packaging that is easy to use (pumps over squeeze), leakproof for travel, and does not create waste or inconvenience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Do you prefer packaging that looks: A: Minimalist
[REAL] Q: What, if anything, frustrates you or feels 'just okay' about your current body wash? A: Refill packs are not economical
[REAL] Q: If you could design your PERFECT body wash from scratch... A: ...foams directly via dispenser... leakproof for travel...
nodes.predictive_value: Innovations in packaging that improve ease-of-use, reduce mess, or simplify dispensing will be highly appealing, while bulky or complicated packaging will be a deterrent.

nodes.label: socially-conscious-in-close-quarters
nodes.description: Becomes more conscious of personal scent and grooming in social situations involving close physical proximity, driven by consideration for others.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: When you think about how your scent affects others, is there a particular situation where you're more conscious of it... A: Social gatherings with close contact where body odor may bother others

[BRANCH] Q: You're scrolling through Instagram and see two ads for body washes... Which ad are you more likely to engage with... A: ...Ad A with the celebrity at the luxury party... appeals to me for social gatherings
nodes.predictive_value: Marketing that depicts intimate social settings (parties, dates, close-knit gatherings) will resonate more and trigger consideration for a product upgrade.

nodes.label: trusts-peer-verification-over-popularity
nodes.description: Values the recommendation of a single trusted, knowledgeable friend over broad popularity metrics or influencer endorsements.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: A: ['doctor_rec','friend_rec','cheaper']
[SYNTHETIC] Q: Scenario: Two friends independently recommend two different body wash brands... Whose recommendation carries more weight? A: B) Friend B — I trust their thoroughness and knowledge.
[SYNTHETIC] Q: You have ₹500 to spend on body wash as gifts for 3 friends... How would you allocate it? A: B) Pick 3 different body washes tailored to each friend's personality.
nodes.predictive_value: A referral program or campaign that leverages authentic peer-to-peer recommendations from trusted individuals will be more effective than one based on mass influencer marketing.

nodes.label: solution-seeker-for-specific-skin-context
nodes.description: Open to trying new products when they promise a specific, tangible solution to a recognized problem (e.g., dryness, hard water, monsoon humidity).
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Q: Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? A: {'rating':4,'explanation':''}
[REAL] Q: What specific benefits or features would make a body wash for Indian skin types and hard water more appealing to you? A: Foams well, does not dry skin
[SYNTHETIC] Q: During monsoon season, you need a body wash that specifically addresses humidity-related skin concerns... Would you trust a recommendation from: ... A) a dermatologist's Instagram reel
